steps.title: Translate the input x = 5
steps.content: In $P(x) = 533(1.011)^x$, $x$ is the number of years after 1951. So $x = 5$ corresponds to the year $1951 + 5 = 1956$.

steps.title: Translate the output P(5) ≈ 563
steps.content: $P(x)$ is the modeled population. Therefore $P(5) \approx 563$ says the population in 1956 is approximately 563.

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A says the population grew by about 5 from 1951 to 1956, confusing the input $x = 5$ with the output.
  • B says the population is about 563 in 1956 — this matches the interpretation of $P(5) \approx 563$.
  • C says the 1956 population is 5 times the 1951 population, but $P(0) = 533$ and $563/533 \approx 1.06$, not 5.
  • D describes a constant increase of 563 every 5 years, but the model is exponential, not linear, and 563 is a population value, not an increment.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
